1	from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
2	
3	from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send
4	
5	
6	# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
7	# in their own AsyncExitStack
8	class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
9	    def __init__(
10	        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
11	    ) -> None:
12	        self.app = app
13	        self.context_name = context_name
14	
15	    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
16	        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
17	            scope[self.context_name] = stack
18	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
19

1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["pdm-backend"]

5	[project]
6	name = "fastapi"
7	dynamic = ["version"]
8	description = "FastAPI framework, high performance, easy to learn, fast to code, ready for production"
9	readme = "README.md"
10	license = "MIT"
11	license-files = ["LICENSE"]
12	requires-python = ">=3.10"
13	authors = [
14	    { name = "Sebastián Ramírez", email = "tiangolo@gmail.com" },
15	]
16	classifiers = [
17	    "Intended Audience :: Information Technology",
18	    "Intended Audience :: System Administrators",
19	    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
20	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
21	    "Programming Language :: Python",
22	    "Topic :: Internet",
23	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks",
24	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules",
25	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries",
26	    "Topic :: Software Development",
27	    "Typing :: Typed",
28	    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
29	    "Environment :: Web Environment",
30	    "Framework :: AsyncIO",
31	    "Framework :: FastAPI",
32	    "Framework :: Pydantic",
33	    "Framework :: Pydantic :: 2",
34	    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
35	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only",
36	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
37	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
38	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
39	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
40	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
41	    "Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP :: HTTP Servers",
42	    "Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP",
43	]
44	dependencies = [
45	    "starlette>=0.46.0",
46	    "pydantic>=2.9.0",
47	    "typing-extensions>=4.8.0",
48	    "typing-inspection>=0.4.2",
49	    "annotated-doc>=0.0.2",
50	]
51	
52	[project.urls]
53	Homepage = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi"
54	Documentation = "https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/"
55	Repository = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi"
56	Issues = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues"
57	Changelog = "https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/release-notes/"
58	
59	[project.optional-dependencies]
60	standard = [
61	    "fastapi-cli[standard] >=0.0.8",
62	    # For the test client
63	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
64	    # For templates
65	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
66	    # For forms and file uploads
67	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
68	    # To validate email fields
69	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
70	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
71	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
72	    # # Settings management
73	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
74	    # # Extra Pydantic data types
75	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
76	]
77	
78	standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli = [
79	    "fastapi-cli[standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli] >=0.0.8",
80	    # For the test client
81	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
82	    # For templates
83	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
84	    # For forms and file uploads
85	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
86	    # To validate email fields
87	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
88	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
89	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
90	    # # Settings management
91	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
92	    # # Extra Pydantic data types
93	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
94	]
95	
96	all = [
97	    "fastapi-cli[standard] >=0.0.8",
98	    # # For the test client
99	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
100	    # For templates
101	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
102	    # For forms and file uploads
103	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
104	    # For Starlette's SessionMiddleware, not commonly used with FastAPI
105	    "itsdangerous >=1.1.0",
106	    # For Starlette's schema generation, would not be used with FastAPI
107	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1",
108	    # To validate email fields
109	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
110	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
111	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
112	    # Settings management
113	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
114	    # Extra Pydantic data types
115	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
116	]
117	
118	[project.scripts]
119	fastapi = "fastapi.cli:main"

185	    "pytest-cov>=4.0.0",
186	    "pytest-sugar>=1.0.0",
187	    "pytest-timeout>=2.4.0",
188	]
189	translations = [

191	    "pydantic-ai >=0.4.10",
192	    "pygithub >=2.8.1",
193	]

195	[tool.pdm]
196	version = { source = "file", path = "fastapi/__init__.py" }
197	distribution = true
198	
199	[tool.pdm.build]
200	source-includes = [
201	    "tests/",
202	    "docs_src/",
203	    "scripts/",
204	    # For a test
205	    "docs/en/docs/img/favicon.png",
206	]
207	
208	[tool.mypy]
209	plugins = ["pydantic.mypy"]
210	strict = true
211	
212	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
213	module = "fastapi.concurrency"
214	warn_unused_ignores = false
215	ignore_missing_imports = true
216	
217	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
218	module = "fastapi.tests.*"
219	ignore_missing_imports = true
220	check_untyped_defs = true
221	
222	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
223	module = "docs_src.*"
224	disallow_incomplete_defs = false
225	disallow_untyped_defs = false
226	disallow_untyped_calls = false
227	
228	[tool.pytest]
229	minversion = "9.0"
230	addopts = [
231	  "--strict-config",
232	  "--strict-markers",
233	  "--ignore=docs_src",
234	]
235	strict_xfail = true
236	filterwarnings = [
237	    "error",
238	]
239	timeout = "20"
240	
241	[tool.coverage.run]
242	parallel = true
243	data_file = "coverage/.coverage"
244	source = [
245	    "docs_src",
246	    "tests",
247	    "fastapi"
248	]
249	relative_files = true
250	context = '${CONTEXT}'

262	]
263	
264	[tool.coverage.report]
265	show_missing = true
266	sort = "-Cover"
267	
268	[tool.coverage.html]
269	show_contexts = true
270

11	
12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55	
56	@router.post("/middleware")
57	def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
58	    return {"message": "OK"}

60	
61	app.include_router(router)
62	app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)

11	
12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55	
56	@router.post("/middleware")
57	def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
58	    return {"message": "OK"}
59

62	app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)
63	
64

72

74	        with open(path, "rb") as file:
75	            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
76	        assert response.status_code == 422, response.text
77	        assert response.json() == {
78	            "detail": {
79	                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
80	                "code": 999,
81	                "message": "File limit exceeded",
82	            }
83	        }

9	def app():

15	    )

18	    async def root():

33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
92	        body = response.json()
93	        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
94	        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
95	        assert body["retry_after"] > 0
96	
97	    def test_custom_limits(self):
98	        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
99	        app = FastAPI()
100	        app.add_middleware(
101	            RateLimitMiddleware,
102	            requests_per_minute=120,
103	            burst_size=2,
104	        )
105	
106	        @app.get("/")
107	        async def root():
108	            return {"ok": True}
109	
110	        client = TestClient(app)
111	        # Burst of 2
112	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
113	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
114	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

10	class State(BaseModel):
11	    app_startup: bool = False
12	    app_shutdown: bool = False
13	    router_startup: bool = False
14	    router_shutdown: bool = False
15	    sub_router_startup: bool = False
16	    sub_router_shutdown: bool = False
17	
18	
19	@pytest.fixture
20	def state() -> State:
21	    return State()
22	
23	
24	@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(
25	    r"ignore:\s*on_event is deprecated, use lifespan event handlers instead.*:DeprecationWarning"
26	)
27	def test_router_events(state: State) -> None:
28	    app = FastAPI()
29	
30	    @app.get("/")
31	    def main() -> dict[str, str]:
32	        return {"message": "Hello World"}
33	
34	    @app.on_event("startup")
35	    def app_startup() -> None:
36	        state.app_startup = True
37	
38	    @app.on_event("shutdown")
39	    def app_shutdown() -> None:
40	        state.app_shutdown = True
41	
42	    router = APIRouter()
43	
44	    @router.on_event("startup")
45	    def router_startup() -> None:
46	        state.router_startup = True
47	
48	    @router.on_event("shutdown")
49	    def router_shutdown() -> None:
50	        state.router_shutdown = True
51	
52	    sub_router = APIRouter()
53	
54	    @sub_router.on_event("startup")
55	    def sub_router_startup() -> None:
56	        state.sub_router_startup = True
57	
58	    @sub_router.on_event("shutdown")
59	    def sub_router_shutdown() -> None:
60	        state.sub_router_shutdown = True
61	
62	    router.include_router(sub_router)
63	    app.include_router(router)
64	
65	    assert state.app_startup is False
66	    assert state.router_startup is False
67	    assert state.sub_router_startup is False
68	    assert state.app_shutdown is False
69	    assert state.router_shutdown is False
70	    assert state.sub_router_shutdown is False
71	    with TestClient(app) as client:
72	        assert state.app_startup is True
73	        assert state.router_startup is True
74	        assert state.sub_router_startup is True
75	        assert state.app_shutdown is False
76	        assert state.router_shutdown is False
77	        assert state.sub_router_shutdown is False
78	        response = client.get("/")
79	        assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
80	        assert response.json() == {"message": "Hello World"}
81	    assert state.app_startup is True
82	    assert state.router_startup is True
83	    assert state.sub_router_startup is True
84	    assert state.app_shutdown is True
85	    assert state.router_shutdown is True
86	    assert state.sub_router_shutdown is True
87	
88	
89	def test_app_lifespan_state(state: State) -> None:
90	    @asynccontextmanager
91	    async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncGenerator[None, None]:
92	        state.app_startup = True
93	        yield
94	        state.app_shutdown = True
95	
96	    app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
97	
98	    @app.get("/")
99	    def main() -> dict[str, str]:
100	        return {"message": "Hello World"}

902	                FastAPI will generate OpenAPI version 3.1.0, and will output that as
903	                the OpenAPI version. But some tools, even though they might be
904	                compatible with OpenAPI 3.1.0, might not recognize it as a valid.
905	
906	                So you could override this value to trick those tools into using
907	                the generated OpenAPI. Have in mind that this is a hack. But if you
908	                avoid using features added in OpenAPI 3.1.0, it might work for your
909	                use case.
910	
911	                This is not passed as a parameter to the `FastAPI` class to avoid
912	                giving the false idea that FastAPI would generate a different OpenAPI
913	                schema. It is only available as an attribute.
914	
915	                **Example**
916	
917	                ```python
918	                from fastapi import FastAPI
919	
920	                app = FastAPI()
921	
922	                app.openapi_version = "3.0.2"
923	                ```
924	                """
925	            ),
926	        ] = "3.1.0"
927	        self.openapi_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None
928	        if self.openapi_url:
929	            assert self.title, "A title must be provided for OpenAPI, e.g.: 'My API'"
930	            assert self.version, "A version must be provided for OpenAPI, e.g.: '2.1.0'"
931	        # TODO: remove when discarding the openapi_prefix parameter
932	        if openapi_prefix:
933	            logger.warning(
934	                '"openapi_prefix" has been deprecated in favor of "root_path", which '
935	                "follows more closely the ASGI standard, is simpler, and more "
936	                "automatic. Check the docs at "
937	                "https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/sub-applications/"
938	            )
939	        self.webhooks: Annotated[
940	            routing.APIRouter,
941	            Doc(
942	                """
943	                The `app.webhooks` attribute is an `APIRouter` with the *path
944	                operations* that will be used just for documentation of webhooks.
945	
946	                Read more about it in the
947	                [FastAPI docs for OpenAPI Webhooks](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/openapi-webhooks/).
948	                """
949	            ),
950	        ] = webhooks or routing.APIRouter()
951	        self.root_path = root_path or openapi_prefix
952	        self.state: Annotated[
953	            State,
954	            Doc(
955	                """
956	                A state object for the application. This is the same object for the
957	                entire application, it doesn't change from request to request.
958	
959	                You normally wouldn't use this in FastAPI, for most of the cases you
960	                would instead use FastAPI dependencies.
961	
962	                This is simply inherited from Starlette.
963	
964	                Read more about it in the
965	                [Starlette docs for Applications](https://www.starlette.dev/applications/#storing-state-on-the-app-instance).
966	                """
967	            ),
968	        ] = State()
969	        self.dependency_overrides: Annotated[
970	            dict[Callable[..., Any], Callable[..., Any]],
971	            Doc(
972	                """
973	                A dictionary with overrides for the dependencies.
974	
975	                Each key is the original dependency callable, and the value is the
976	                actual dependency that should be called.
977	
978	                This is for testing, to replace expensive dependencies with testing
979	                versions.
980	
981	                Read more about it in the
982	                [FastAPI docs for Testing Dependencies with Overrides](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/testing-dependencies/).
983	                """
984	            ),
985	        ] = {}
986	        self.router: routing.APIRouter = routing.APIRouter(
987	            routes=routes,
988	            redirect_slashes=redirect_slashes,
989	            dependency_overrides_provider=self,
990	            on_startup=on_startup,
991	            on_shutdown=on_shutdown,
992	            lifespan=lifespan,
993	            default_response_class=default_response_class,
994	            dependencies=dependencies,
995	            callbacks=callbacks,
996	            deprecated=deprecated,
997	            include_in_schema=include_in_schema,
998	            responses=responses,
999	            generate_unique_id_function=generate_unique_id_function,
1000	            strict_content_type=strict_content_type,
1001	        )
1002	        self.exception_handlers: dict[
1003	            Any, Callable[[Request, Any], Response | Awaitable[Response]]
1004	        ] = {} if exception_handlers is None else dict(exception_handlers)
1005	        self.exception_handlers.setdefault(HTTPException, http_exception_handler)
1006	        self.exception_handlers.setdefault(
1007	            RequestValidationError, request_validation_exception_handler
1008	        )
1009	
1010	        # Starlette still has incorrect type specification for the handlers
1011	        self.exception_handlers.setdefault(
1012	            WebSocketRequestValidationError,
1013	            websocket_request_validation_exception_handler,  # type: ignore[arg-type]  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]
1014	        )  # ty: ignore[no-matching-overload]
1015	
1016	        self.user_middleware: list[Middleware] = (
1017	            [] if middleware is None else list(middleware)
1018	        )
1019	        self.middleware_stack: ASGIApp | None = None
1020	        self.setup()
1021	
1022	    def build_middleware_stack(self) -> ASGIApp:
1023	        # Duplicate/override from Starlette to add AsyncExitStackMiddleware
1024	        # inside of ExceptionMiddleware, inside of custom user middlewares
1025	        debug = self.debug
1026	        error_handler = None
1027	        exception_handlers: dict[Any, ExceptionHandler] = {}
1028	
1029	        for key, value in self.exception_handlers.items():
1030	            if key in (500, Exception):
1031	                error_handler = value
1032	            else:
1033	                exception_handlers[key] = value
1034	
1035	        middleware = (
1036	            [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug)]  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1037	            + self.user_middleware
1038	            + [
1039	                Middleware(
1040	                    ExceptionMiddleware,  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1041	                    handlers=exception_handlers,
1042	                    debug=debug,
1043	                ),
1044	                # Add FastAPI-specific AsyncExitStackMiddleware for closing files.
1045	                # Before this was also used for closing dependencies with yield but
1046	                # those now have their own AsyncExitStack, to properly support
1047	                # streaming responses while keeping compatibility with the previous
1048	                # versions (as of writing 0.117.1) that allowed doing
1049	                # except HTTPException inside a dependency with yield.
1050	                # This needs to happen after user middlewares because those create a
1051	                # new contextvars context copy by using a new AnyIO task group.
1052	                # This AsyncExitStack preserves the context for contextvars, not
1053	                # strictly necessary for closing files but it was one of the original
1054	                # intentions.
1055	                # If the AsyncExitStack lived outside of the custom middlewares and
1056	                # contextvars were set, for example in a dependency with 'yield'
1057	                # in that internal contextvars context, the values would not be
1058	                # available in the outer context of the AsyncExitStack.
1059	                # By placing the middleware and the AsyncExitStack here, inside all
1060	                # user middlewares, the same context is used.
1061	                # This is currently not needed, only for closing files, but used to be
1062	                # important when dependencies with yield were closed here.
1063	                Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1064	            ]
1065	        )
1066	
1067	        app = self.router
1068	        for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
1069	            app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)
1070	        return app
1071	
1072	    def openapi(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
1073	        """
1074	        Generate the OpenAPI schema of the application. This is called by FastAPI
1075	        internally.
1076	
1077	        The first time it is called it stores the result in the attribute
1078	        `app.openapi_schema`, and next times it is called, it just returns that same
1079	        result. To avoid the cost of generating the schema every time.
1080	
1081	        If you need to modify the generated OpenAPI schema, you could modify it.
1082	
1083	        Read more in the
1084	        [FastAPI docs for OpenAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/how-to/extending-openapi/).
1085	        """
1086	        if not self.openapi_schema:
1087	            self.openapi_schema = get_openapi(
1088	                title=self.title,
1089	                version=self.version,
1090	                openapi_version=self.openapi_version,
1091	                summary=self.summary,
1092	                description=self.description,
1093	                terms_of_service=self.terms_of_service,
1094	                contact=self.contact,
1095	                license_info=self.license_info,
1096	                routes=self.routes,
1097	                webhooks=self.webhooks.routes,
1098	                tags=self.openapi_tags,
1099	                servers=self.servers,

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the test structure. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## FastAPI Test Patterns Summary

Based on my exploration of the FastAPI codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of the test patterns used:

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Main Tests Directory**: `/tests/`

The tests directory contains:
- **150+ top-level test files** (individual test modules)
- **5 subdirectories**:
  - `benchmarks/` - Performance benchmarking tests
  - `test_modules_same_name_body/` - Special test for modules with the same name
  - `test_validate_response_recursive/` - Response validation tests
  - `test_request_params/` - Request parameter tests with 7 subdirectories:
    - `test_body/`, `test_cookie/`, `test_file/`, `test_form/`, `test_header/`, `test_path/`, `test_query/`
  - `test_tutorial/` - Tutorial-based tests with 78 subdirectories, including:
    - `test_middleware/` - Middleware examples (3 tutorial files)
    - `test_advanced_middleware/` - Advanced middleware (3 tutorial files)
    - Multiple others covering various features

**Utility Files**:
- `/tests/main.py` - Shared test application with comprehensive route definitions
- `/tests/utils.py` - Pytest utilities and markers
- `/tests/__init__.py` - Package marker

### 2. CORE TESTING PATTERNS

#### A. Basic Test Structure (Using TestClient)

All tests use **`FastAPI.testclient.TestClient`** for synchronous testing:

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI()

# ... define routes ...

client = TestClient(app)

def test_something():
    response = client.get("/path")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == expected_data
```

**Key File**: `/tests/test_application.py` (58KB - comprehensive example)
- Demonstrates basic routes, middleware, status codes
- Uses parametrized tests with `@pytest.mark.parametrize`
- Tests Swagger UI, ReDoc, OpenAPI schema validation
- Uses `inline_snapshot` for schema assertion

#### B. Middleware Testing Pattern

**Middleware Tests**: `/tests/test_tutorial/test_middleware/test_tutorial001.py`

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from inline_snapshot import snapshot

from docs_src.middleware.tutorial001_py310 import app

client = TestClient(app)

def test_response_headers():
    response = client.get("/openapi.json")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    assert "X-Process-Time" in response.headers

def test_openapi_schema():
    response = client.get("/openapi.json")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    assert response.json() == snapshot(...)
```

**Advanced Middleware Testing**: `/tests/test_tutorial/test_advanced_middleware/test_tutorial001.py`

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

from docs_src.advanced_middleware.tutorial001_py310 import app

def test_middleware():
    # Test with HTTPS base URL
    client = TestClient(app, base_url="https://testserver")
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text

    # Test redirect behavior
    client = TestClient(app)
    response = client.get("/", follow_redirects=False)
    assert response.status_code == 307, response.text
    assert response.headers["location"] == "https://testserver/"
```

**Custom Middleware Exception Testing**: `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`

- Defines custom middleware class with `__init__` and `__call__` methods
- Uses `app.add_middleware()` to register
- Tests error scenarios with temporary files via `tmp_path` fixture
- Full file content shows ASGI middleware pattern

#### C. Dependency Injection Testing

**Dependency Cache Pattern**: `/tests/test_dependency_cache.py`

```python
from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, Security
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()
counter_holder = {"counter": 0}

async def dep_counter():
    counter_holder["counter"] += 1
    return counter_holder["counter"]

@app.get("/counter/")
async def get_counter(count: int = Depends(dep_counter)):
    return {"counter": count}

client = TestClient(app)

def test_normal_counter():
    counter_holder["counter"] = 0
    response = client.get("/counter/")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"counter": 1}
```

**Dependency Overrides Pattern**: `/tests/test_dependency_overrides.py`

```python
@pytest.fixture
def state() -> State:
    return State()

async def common_parameters(q: str, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
    return {"q": q, "skip": skip, "limit": limit}

@app.get("/main-depends/")
async def main_depends(commons: dict = Depends(common_parameters)):
    return {"in": "main-depends", "params": commons}

def test_main_depends_q_foo():
    response = client.get("/main-depends/?q=foo")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {
        "in": "main-depends",
        "params": {"q": "foo", "skip": 0, "limit": 100},
    }
```

#### D. WebSocket Testing

**Pattern**: `/tests/test_ws_router.py`

```python
from fastapi import WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect

app = FastAPI()

async def index(websocket: WebSocket):

async def router_ws_decorator_depends(
    websocket: WebSocket, data=Depends(ws_dependency)
):
    await websocket.accept()
    await websocket.send_text(data)
    await websocket.close()

with client.websocket_connect("/") as websocket:
```

#### E. Exception Handler Testing

**Pattern**: `/tests/test_exception_handlers.py`

```python
from fastapi import HTTPException, FastAPI
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse

def http_exception_handler(request, exception):
    return JSONResponse({"exception": "http-exception"})

def request_validation_exception_handler(request, exception):
    return JSONResponse({"exception": "request-validation"})

app = FastAPI(
    exception_handlers={
        HTTPException: http_exception_handler,
        RequestValidationError: request_validation_exception_handler,
        Exception: server_error_exception_handler,
    }
)

client = TestClient(app)

def test_override_http_exception():
    response = client.get("/http-exception")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"exception": "http-exception"}

def test_override_server_error_exception_response():
    # raise_server_exceptions=False catches exceptions
    client = TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False)
    response = client.get("/server-error")
    assert response.status_code == 500
    assert response.json() == {"exception": "server-error"}
```

#### F. Server-Sent Events (SSE) Testing

**Pattern**: `/tests/test_sse.py`

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, APIRouter
from fastapi.responses import EventSourceResponse
from fastapi.sse import ServerSentEvent
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()

@app.get("/items/stream", response_class=EventSourceResponse)
async def sse_items() -> AsyncIterable[Item]:
    for item in items:
        yield item

@pytest.fixture(name="client")
def client_fixture():
    with TestClient(app) as c:
        yield c

def test_async_generator_with_model(client: TestClient):
    response = client.get("/items/stream")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.headers["content-type"] == "text/event-stream; charset=utf-8"
    
    lines = response.text.strip().split("\n")
    data_lines = [line for line in lines if line.startswith("data: ")]
    assert len(data_lines) == 3

def test_sse_events_with_fields(client: TestClient):
    response = client.get("/items/stream-sse-event")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    text = response.text
    assert "event: greeting\n" in text
    assert 'data: "hello"\n' in text
    assert "id: 1\n" in text
```

#### G. Lifespan/Events Testing

**Pattern**: `/tests/test_router_events.py`

```python
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator
from fastapi import FastAPI

@pytest.fixture
def state() -> State:
    return State()

@pytest.mark.filterwarnings(
    r"ignore:\s*on_event is deprecated, use lifespan event handlers instead.*:DeprecationWarning"
)
def test_router_events(state: State) -> None:
    app = FastAPI()

    @app.on_event("startup")
    def app_startup() -> None:
        state.app_startup = True

    @app.on_event("shutdown")
    def app_shutdown() -> None:
        state.app_shutdown = True

    router = APIRouter()

    @router.on_event("startup")
    def router_startup() -> None:
        state.router_startup = True

    with TestClient(app) as client:
        assert state.app_startup is True
        assert state.router_startup is True
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 200
    assert state.app_shutdown is True

def test_app_lifespan_state(state: State) -> None:
    @asynccontextmanager
    async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncGenerator[None, None]:
        state.app_startup = True
        yield
        state.app_shutdown = True

    app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)

    @app.get("/")
    def main() -> dict[str, str]:
        return {"message": "Hello World"}
```

### 3. TEST UTILITIES

**File**: `/tests/utils.py`


**Assertions**:

```python
assert response.json() == expected_dict
assert response.text == "expected text"
assert "substring" in response.text
```

### 5. SHARED TEST APPLICATION PATTERN

A comprehensive test app used by `/tests/test_application.py` with:
- Multiple route types (GET, POST, etc.)

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the test structure. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## FastAPI Test Patterns Summary

Based on my exploration of the FastAPI codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of the test patterns used:

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Main Tests Directory**: `/tests/`

The tests directory contains:
- **150+ top-level test files** (individual test modules)
- **5 subdirectories**:
  - `benchmarks/` - Performance benchmarking tests
  - `test_modules_same_name_body/` - Special test for modules with the same name
  - `test_validate_response_recursive/` - Response validation tests
  - `test_request_params/` - Request parameter tests with 7 subdirectories:
    - `test_body/`, `test_cookie/`, `test_file/`, `test_form/`, `test_header/`, `test_path/`, `test_query/`
  - `test_tutorial/` - Tutorial-based tests with 78 subdirectories, including:
    - `test_middleware/` - Middleware examples (3 tutorial files)
    - `test_advanced_middleware/` - Advanced middleware (3 tutorial files)
    - Multiple others covering various features

**Utility Files**:
- `/tests/main.py` - Shared test application with comprehensive route definitions
- `/tests/utils.py` - Pytest utilities and markers
- `/tests/__init__.py` - Package marker

### 2. CORE TESTING PATTERNS

#### A. Basic Test Structure (Using TestClient)

All tests use **`FastAPI.testclient.TestClient`** for synchronous testing:

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI()

# ... define routes ...

client = TestClient(app)

def test_something():
    response = client.get("/path")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == expected_data
```

**Key File**: `/tests/test_application.py` (58KB - comprehensive example)
- Demonstrates basic routes, middleware, status codes
- Uses parametrized tests with `@pytest.mark.parametrize`
- Tests Swagger UI, ReDoc, OpenAPI schema validation
- Uses `inline_snapshot` for schema assertion

#### B. Middleware Testing Pattern

**Middleware Tests**: `/tests/test_tutorial/test_middleware/test_tutorial001.py`

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from inline_snapshot import snapshot

from docs_src.middleware.tutorial001_py310 import app

client = TestClient(app)

def test_response_headers():
    response = client.get("/openapi.json")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    assert "X-Process-Time" in response.headers

def test_openapi_schema():
    response = client.get("/openapi.json")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    assert response.json() == snapshot(...)
```

**Advanced Middleware Testing**: `/tests/test_tutorial/test_advanced_middleware/test_tutorial001.py`

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

from docs_src.advanced_middleware.tutorial001_py310 import app

def test_middleware():
    # Test with HTTPS base URL
    client = TestClient(app, base_url="https://testserver")
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text

    # Test redirect behavior
    client = TestClient(app)
    response = client.get("/", follow_redirects=False)
    assert response.status_code == 307, response.text
    assert response.headers["location"] == "https://testserver/"
```

**Custom Middleware Exception Testing**: `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`

- Defines custom middleware class with `__init__` and `__call__` methods
- Uses `app.add_middleware()` to register
- Tests error scenarios with temporary files via `tmp_path` fixture
- Full file content shows ASGI middleware pattern

#### C. Dependency Injection Testing

**Dependency Cache Pattern**: `/tests/test_dependency_cache.py`

```python

    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"counter": 1}
```

## Comprehensive FastAPI/Starlette Middleware Implementation Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the FastAPI codebase, here's a detailed overview of how middleware is implemented:

### 1. Middleware Directory Structure

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775321401377-89gxta/fastapi/middleware/`

**Files Present:**
- `__init__.py` - Module exports
- `asyncexitstack.py` - FastAPI-specific AsyncExitStack middleware
- `cors.py` - CORS middleware (re-exported from Starlette)
- `gzip.py` - GZip middleware (re-exported from Starlette)
- `httpsredirect.py` - HTTPS redirect middleware (re-exported from Starlette)
- `trustedhost.py` - Trusted host middleware (re-exported from Starlette)
- `wsgi.py` - WSGI middleware (re-exported from Starlette)

### 2. Full File Contents

#### `/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```

#### `/fastapi/middleware/cors.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware  # noqa
```

#### `/fastapi/middleware/gzip.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware as GZipMiddleware  # noqa
```

#### `/fastapi/middleware/httpsredirect.py`
```python
from starlette.middleware.httpsredirect import (  # noqa
    HTTPSRedirectMiddleware as HTTPSRedirectMiddleware,
)
```

from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

app = FastAPI()

router = APIRouter()


class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
    Args:
      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
    """

    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
        received = 0

        async def inner():
            nonlocal received
            message = await receive()
            if message["type"] != "http.request":
                return message  # pragma: no cover

            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
            received += body_len
            if received > self.max_content_size:
                raise HTTPException(
                    422,
                    detail={
                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                        "code": 999,
                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
                    },
                )
            return message

        return inner

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)


@router.post("/middleware")
def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
    return {"message": "OK"}


app.include_router(router)
app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)


client = TestClient(app)


def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
    default_pydantic_max_size = 2**16
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (default_pydantic_max_size + 1))

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 422, response.text
        assert response.json() == {
            "detail": {
                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
                "code": 999,
                "message": "File limit exceeded",
            }
        }


def test_custom_middleware_exception_not_raised(tmp_path: Path):
    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
    path.write_bytes(b"<file content>")

    with client:
        with open(path, "rb") as file:
            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
        assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
        assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}

## Key Observations:

1. **Middleware pattern**: FastAPI uses Starlette's ASGI middleware pattern. The primary pattern in this codebase is a class with `__init__(self, app: ASGIApp, ...)` and `async __call__(self, scope, receive, send)` methods.

2. **Other middleware files**: Most middleware files in `fastapi/middleware/` are just re-exports from Starlette (cors.py, gzip.py, etc.). 

3. **`asyncexitstack.py`** is a native implementation, unlike the re-exports.

4. **`__init__.py`** just exports `Middleware` from starlette.

5. **Tests directory**: There's no `tests/test_apps/` directory yet - we'll need to create it.

6. **Testing patterns**: Use `TestClient` from `fastapi.testclient`.

## Plan:

### File 1: `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py`

I'll implement `RateLimitMiddleware` using the token bucket algorithm, where each IP gets a bucket with `burst_size` tokens that refill at a configured rate.

**Token Bucket Algorithm**:
- Tokens refill at `requests_per_minute / 60` tokens per second
- Each request consumes 1 token; if empty, return 429
- Track per-IP state with `(last_refill_time, current_tokens)`

**Implementation approach**: 
- Use `asyncio.Lock` per IP for thread safety
- Store bucket state per IP in a `dict`
- Extract client IP from `scope["client"]` (a `(host, port)` tuple in ASGI)

**Response headers** on all responses:
- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: requests_per_minute
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: floor of current tokens after consuming
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: seconds until next full token refill

**429 response**:
- Return JSON with `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": <seconds>}`
- Still setting up the response...

For the ASGI implementation, I need to intercept the `send` callable to add headers to every response, including 429s. I'll check the rate limit first, and if exceeded, send the 429 directly. Otherwise, I'll wrap `send` to inject the rate limit headers into the `http.response.start` event before passing control to the app. For the remaining tokens header, I'm calculating it as the floor of tokens after consumption.

Implementing the token bucket middleware... Writing rate limit logic... Still writing middleware logic...

Actually, I need to reconsider how I'm formatting the `retry_after` value — the spec expects it as a number in seconds, so I should use `int(math.ceil(retry_after))` to round up properly. For the `X-RateLimit-Reset` header, I'm interpreting it as the seconds until the bucket refills or until the next request could succeed, which should align with when capacity is restored.

Now I'm thinking through the middleware setup and test structure. I need to update the imports to expose the rate limit middleware properly, and then set up comprehensive tests to verify the behavior.

For the test app, I'll configure it with tight limits like `requests_per_minute=60, burst_size=3` so I can easily trigger rate limit responses and validate that the 429 status code, retry headers, and JSON error details all work correctly. So the 4th request gets blocked with a 429 since there are no tokens left and none have refilled. Now I'm setting up a test file with the FastAPI app and middleware configured, then writing test cases to verify the rate limit headers are present and the blocking behavior works correctly.

I'm realizing there's a state management issue though—since the app and client are defined at module level, multiple tests will share the same rate limit bucket, which means test isolation breaks down. I need to either create fresh fixtures for each test or find a way to reset the client's IP address, since TestClient defaults to 127.0.0.1 and I can't easily override that in the ASGI scope.

The cleanest solution is to use pytest fixtures that spin up a fresh app and client for each test function, ensuring the rate limit state starts clean every time.

Setting up fixtures...

Now I'm planning out the test cases I need to cover: verifying the rate limit headers appear correctly, checking that the remaining count decrements properly with each request, confirming that requests get rejected with a 429 status once the burst limit is exceeded, and validating the JSON response structure when rate limited.

I should also test that the 429 response includes the rate limit headers, optionally verify that non-HTTP protocols like WebSocket aren't affected, and ensure the middleware respects custom configuration values. For the file structure, I need to create the middleware implementation, set up a test app with the middleware configured, and update the middleware exports—I should also check whether the test subdirectory needs an `__init__.py` file by looking at the existing test structure.

Looking at how other test directories are organized, subdirectories like `tests/test_tutorial/test_middleware/` do have `__init__.py` files, so I'll need one in the new `tests/test_apps/` directory too. There's also a potential issue with `asyncio.Lock()` being instantiated in the middleware's `__init__` method—in Python 3.10 and later this isn't a problem since the deprecation warning about needing a running event loop was removed, but I should keep this in mind for compatibility. and the project targets Python 3.10+ based on the test file naming conventions. I'm going to stick with initializing `asyncio.Lock()` directly in `__init__` since that's the cleanest approach and aligns with the project's version requirements.

Now I need to figure out what value to use for `retry_after` in the rate limit response body.  I'll use `math.ceil()` to convert it to a whole number of seconds. For the `X-RateLimit-Reset` header, I'm deciding between two interpretations: either the time until one token is available again, or the time until the bucket is completely full. I think "seconds until the next request can be served" makes more sense, so I'll calculate that as `(1 - tokens) / rate` when tokens are below 1.

For successful requests where tokens remain, I'll simplify this to just computing the time until the next token arrives using the fractional part of the current tokens. I'm working through the logic for when tokens fall below 1 after a request—if there's no token available, the reset time should be calculated as the ceiling of the time needed to refill to 1 token. Let me trace through the test case: with a burst size of 3 and a rate-based refill, the first two requests succeed immediately since tokens remain >= 1, but the third request depletes the bucket entirely, requiring a reset time based on the refill rate. I'm finishing the formula for the retry_after calculation, then moving on to finalize the implementation plan. I need to verify the test directory structure has the right `__init__.py` files, and I'm also considering an edge case where the client IP might be None in some ASGI environments—I'll default those to "unknown" so they all share the same rate limit bucket. Now I'm ready to write out the complete plan for the rate limiter middleware. 2. **`tests/test_apps/__init__.py`** - Empty init file for new test package
3. **`tests/test_apps/test_rate_limit_app.py`** - Demo app + tests

### Files to Modify:
1. **`fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`** - Export `RateLimitMiddleware`

### Token Bucket Algorithm:
- Per-IP state: `(last_refill_time, current_tokens)`
- On each request:
  1. Calculate elapsed time since last refill
  2. Add `elapsed * rate` tokens, capped at `burst_size`
  3. If tokens >= 1: consume 1 token, allow request
  4. Else: return 429

### ASGI Implementation Pattern:
- Class with `__init__(self, app: ASGIApp, ...)` and `async __call__(self, scope, receive, send)`
- Intercept the `send` callable to inject rate limit headers into the response, and for 429 responses, send them directly without passing through to the app.

Now I'll write out the plan file.

Design a detailed implementation plan for a `RateLimitMiddleware` in a FastAPI codebase. Here's the full context:

## Repository Root
`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775321401377-89gxta/`

## Existing Middleware Pattern

The existing custom middleware (from `fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py`) follows the pure ASGI pattern:
```python
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

All other middleware files are re-exports from Starlette:
- `fastapi/middleware/cors.py`: `from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware  # noqa`
- `fastapi/middleware/gzip.py`: same pattern

`fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`:
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```

## Test Patterns

Tests use `TestClient` from `fastapi.testclient`. Fixtures are used to create fresh app instances per test to avoid state leakage. Example fixture pattern:
```python
@pytest.fixture()
def client():
    app = FastAPI()
    app.add_middleware(MyMiddleware, ...)
    @app.get("/")
    async def root(): return {}
    return TestClient(app)
```

The `tests/test_apps/` directory does NOT currently exist.

## Requirements

Create:
1. **`fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py`** — `RateLimitMiddleware` using token bucket algorithm:
   - Config: `requests_per_minute: int = 60`, `burst_size: int = 10`
   - Per-client-IP rate limiting (IP from `scope["client"][0]`)
   - HTTP 429 with JSON `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": <seconds>}`
   - Headers on ALL responses: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`
   
2. **`fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`** — add `RateLimitMiddleware` export

3. **`tests/test_apps/__init__.py`** — empty package init

4. **`tests/test_apps/test_rate_limit_app.py`** — demo app + tests for the middleware

## Token Bucket Algorithm Details

- Each IP bucket starts with `burst_size` tokens
- Tokens refill at `requests_per_minute / 60.0` tokens per second (capped at `burst_size`)
- Each request consumes 1 token
- If no tokens available: 429
- Bucket state: `dict[str, tuple[float, float]]` mapping IP → `(last_refill_time, current_tokens)`
- Use `asyncio.Lock()` for concurrency safety

## ASGI Send Wrapping

To add headers to ALL responses, wrap the `send` callable:
```python
async def send_with_headers(message):
    if message["type"] == "http.response.start":
        headers = list(message.get("headers", []))
        headers.append((b"x-ratelimit-limit", str(rpm).encode()))
        # ... add other headers
        message = {**message, "headers": headers}
    await send(message)
await self.app(scope, receive, send_with_headers)
```

For 429 short-circuit, send directly:
```python
await send({"type": "http.response.start", "status": 429, "headers": [...]})
await send({"type": "http.response.body", "body": body_bytes})
```

Please produce a complete, step-by-step implementation plan with:
- Exact code for each file
- Notes on edge cases (unknown IP, non-HTTP scopes, reset calculation)
- Test cases that verify the token bucket behavior deterministically
- How `retry_after` (int seconds, ceiling) and `X-RateLimit-Reset` (int seconds) should be calculated

40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
92	        body = response.json()
93	        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
94	        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
95	        assert body["retry_after"] > 0
96	
97	    def test_custom_limits(self):
98	        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
99	        app = FastAPI()
100	        app.add_middleware(
101	            RateLimitMiddleware,
102	            requests_per_minute=120,
103	            burst_size=2,
104	        )
105	
106	        @app.get("/")
107	        async def root():
108	            return {"ok": True}
109	
110	        client = TestClient(app)
111	        # Burst of 2
112	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
113	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
114	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429
115

3	from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, File, UploadFile
4	from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
5	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
6	
7	app = FastAPI()
8	
9	router = APIRouter()

11	
12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55	
56	@router.post("/middleware")
57	def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
58	    return {"message": "OK"}
59	
60	
61	app.include_router(router)
62	app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)
63	
64	
65	client = TestClient(app)
66	
67	
68	def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
69	    default_pydantic_max_size = 2**16
70	    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
71	    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (default_pydantic_max_size + 1))
72	
73	    with client:
74	        with open(path, "rb") as file:
75	            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
76	        assert response.status_code == 422, response.text
77	        assert response.json() == {
78	            "detail": {
79	                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
80	                "code": 999,
81	                "message": "File limit exceeded",
82	            }
83	        }
84

86	def test_custom_middleware_exception_not_raised(tmp_path: Path):
87	    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"

89	
90	    with client:

92	            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
93	        assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
94	        assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}
